examples.title: Morphological Dilation
examples.description: Implementation of binary image dilation using structuring element
examples.code:
PROCEDURE Dilate(image A, structuringElement B)
 result ← zeros(size(A))
 origin ← getOrigin(B)
 
 FOR each pixel (i,j) in A DO
 IF A[i,j] == 1 THEN
 FOR each pixel (k,l) in B DO
 ni ← i + k - origin.x
 nj ← j + l - origin.y
 IF isValid(ni, nj) THEN
 result[ni,nj] ← 1
 END IF
 END FOR
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 RETURN result
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: For each foreground pixel, place the structuring element with its origin at that pixel and mark all corresponding positions in result as foreground. This expands the object by the SE shape.

examples.title: Hit-or-Miss Transform
examples.description: Pattern detection using simultaneous foreground and background matching
examples.explanation: The hit-or-miss transform finds pixel locations where the foreground pattern (B1) matches the image AND the background pattern (B2) also matches. The two structuring elements must be non-overlapping.

examples.title: Pattern Classification by Prototype Matching
examples.description: Classifies image patterns using morphological similarity to prototype templates
examples.code:
PROCEDURE ClassifyPattern(image A, prototypeSet P, threshold T)
 classes ← getUniqueClasses(P)
 scores ← initialize(classes, 0)
 
 FOR each prototype p in P DO
 // Compute hit-or-miss score
 hits ← CountPixels(HitOrMiss(A, p.foreground, p.background))
 total ← CountPixels(p.foreground) + CountPixels(p.background)
 similarity ← hits / total
 
 // Add to class score
 class ← p.classLabel
 scores[class] ← scores[class] + similarity
 END FOR
 
 // Assign to class with highest score
 bestClass ← argmax(scores)
 
 IF scores[bestClass] >= threshold THEN
 RETURN bestClass
 ELSE
 RETURN "Unknown"
 END IF
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: Multiple prototypes per class improve classification accuracy. Each prototype generates a similarity score via hit-or-miss. Scores accumulate per class, and the pattern is assigned to the highest-scoring class if above threshold.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n² × m² × p) where p = number of prototypes
